NY POWER AGENCY GIVES $1 MILLION TO LEADERS' PET PROJECTS, CHARITIES

Michelle Breidenbach The (Syracuse, N.Y.) Post-Standard January/February 2007
Government & PoliticsBusiness & Economy

An investigation into the New York Power Authority discovered that $1 million in annual donations to executives' favored charities were being funded by taxpayers, revealing serious accountability and transparency issues within the state agency. The probe exposed unethical practices including misuse of public funds and excessive executive perks through analysis of public records and financial audits.

An investigation into the New York Power Authority revealed $1 million in donations annually to executives' favored charities, funded by taxpayer money. It illuminated issues of accountability and transparency within state-run agencies and exposed unethical practices among executives, including misuse of public funds and extensive perks. Using public records and financial audits, Breidenbach highlighted discrepancies in operational and ethical standards.
